subject: SOCDS 2025/26 Update Week #7 - PreK Orange
text:
Email Ms. Annemarie
Walking Field Trip to the Firehouse -
Great Success
Your children were so excited to walk to the firehouse and meet the firefighters! They did an excellent job walking with their partners, listening carefully to their teachers, and staying safe together.
At the firehouse, the firefighters spoke about fire safety and demonstrated their equipment. They even put on their full gear to show the children what they look like during an emergency—reminding everyone not to be afraid if a firefighter ever comes into their home to help.
The highlight of the trip was when each child had the chance to sit in the driver’s seat of the fire engine—what a thrill!
A big thank-you 
to
SOCDS PEP
for providing snacks for the firefighters. Your thoughtfulness was truly appreciated.
MEND Food Drive
Thank you to every family who participated in our annual food drive. Together, we collected more than
30 bags
of food and other nonperishable items! 
The
shelf-stable milk and cooking oil
were especially appreciated by MEND.
Your generosity shows our children what it means to be part of a caring community—one that looks out for others and helps ensure everyone has what they need.
Gardening with Ms. Linda
We’ve been enjoying the crisp fall weather as we continue our study of trees. We learned that there are many kinds of maple trees. We looked carefully at the leaves of three common varieties that are growing at SOCDS: Sugar, Red and Japanese maple trees. We also compared two types of Oak trees (Pin Oak and Black Oak) by comparing the shape 
of their leaves, trunks, branching habits and seeds. We learned that Oak trees are the superheroes of the tree world, in fact they are a keystone species. In North America, they support more life forms including fungi, insects, birds and mammals than any other tree genus. We also looked at several tree seedlings in the garden and discussed how their thin stems can grow year after year into a huge tree trunk.
It is chilly!
Please dress your child appropriately to be outside for 30 minutes at a time.  Good foot wear and a warm hat are useful as many children find heavier jackets constraining.
If you have any questions or concerns please text (1-646-226-8790) or email learn@socds.com.
Annemarie
Youngsters Parent Teacher Conference Sign Up
Art with Ms. Jenny
Ms. Jenny and the children explored how black can be used to accentuate the other colors of the piece.  Children made cats and pumpkins!
Upcoming

Friday, October 31st - PEP Pizza Cupcake Lunch - Special Wear Pajamas!  (no costumes please)
Tuesday, November 11th - SOCDS Closed in honor of Veterans Day

Logistics
Dress Appropriately for the Weather:
Please dress your child in appropriate gear for the day.  There is no bad weather just poor clothing.
Morning Snack
:  Please send in a morning, and if your child is staying for enrichment please send in an afternoon snack as well.  We generally allow children to choose their food and to listen to their bodies, but if you have any particular preference for what they eat and when, please just label the containers and we will accommodate.  Please remember we are a NUT FREE environment.
Water Bottles
:  Please send in a labeled water bottle with your child each day.  We do make sure that they drink water 
throughout the day.  We have two water fountains which we use to refill the bottles (so they may come home full.)
Parking:
Please remember that Vose Avenue is an active busy street.  Please pull up on the
school side
of Vose in front of the school.  It is safest to go around the block instead of turning around in driveways.  For your child's safety please try to have them leave your car from the sidewalk side of the car.
Please do not block our neighbors driveway, even for a few minutes.
Tuition:
As I receive payments I update the statement and send you a copy.  If you do not receive those from me please let me know.
Hours
Morning Care 7:30-8:30am
Morning Drop off is between 8:30-9am
Afternoon Pick Up is between 3:10- 3:20pm
Extended Day 3:20-5:30/5:45pm
South Orange Country Day School
461 Vose Avenue, South Orange
973.762.6451
